checks.a_analysis: I have carefully compared the text in the JSON output against the provided source PDF. All headings ('Annual Report', 'Contents', 'Secretary's Message', 'Looking Ahead') and paragraphs in the rendered sections (Pages 1, 2, 3, 42, and 43) correspond directly to text found in the original document. There are no signs of hallucinated text or fabricated introductory/concluding remarks.

The document contains limited contact information. I have verified the names (Jim Weaver) and general URL references (NC.gov) against the source. There are no fabricated phone numbers, email addresses, or specific mailing addresses present in the output.

The conversion is incomplete. The JSON explicitly contains an omission marker stating that 38 sections were omitted ('_omitted': '38 sections omitted for token budget'). Comparing the physical page count of the PDF (43 pages) to the sections present in the JSON (only Pages 1, 2, 3, 42, and 43), it is clear that the vast majority of the document's core content—including Enterprise Functions, Boards and Commissions, Budget Report, Goals, and Awards—is entirely missing from the output.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains 43 pages of content, including sections on Enterprise Functions (p. 4), Budget Report (p. 12), Goals (p. 14), and Awards (p. 31).
checks.b_evidence_location: Output JSON, between Section 3 and Section 5

checks.a_analysis: The document does not contain traditional grid tables in the non-omitted sections. The 'Contents' list is rendered as a list, and while it is missing items (see C5), this is a text omission rather than a table row issue. Budget data on page 14 is missing due to the full page omission noted in C3.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The 'Contents' section (Section 2) is missing a significant portion of its text. Specifically, it has omitted all indented sub-items found in the original Table of Contents on Page 2, such as the eight sub-sections under 'Enterprise Functions and Divisions' and the five sub-sections under 'Boards and Commissions'. This constitutes an omission of more than 50% of the items in this specific section.
checks.b_evidence_source: The Table of Contents on Page 2 includes 13 sub-items: 'Architecture and Design' through 'Strategy and Governance' (8 items) and 'Information Technology Strategy Board' through 'N.C. Longitudinal Data System Governance Board' (5 items).
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON list in Section 2 only includes the 12 primary headings and completely omits the 13 sub-items.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: There are minor text discrepancies. On the cover page (Section 1), the specific year of the report ('2021/2022') is missing, which is a critical piece of identifying information for a government report. Additionally, in the signature block of the Secretary's Message (Section 3), the output uses the formal name 'James A. Weaver' while the source text explicitly prints 'Jim Weaver'.
checks.b_evidence_source: The cover page (Page 1) contains the text '2021/2022' at the top right.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the provided JSON output and compared it with the source PDF. I found no instances where text blocks, paragraphs, or sections were duplicated in the conversion. The content appears only as many times as it does in the source document.

The conversion uses H1 tags for main page titles and section headers (e.g., 'Annual Report' on p1, 'Contents' on p2, 'Secretary's Message' on p3). While the hierarchy is relatively flat, there are no instances of skipped heading levels (e.g., H1 to H3). Normalization of heading levels for accessibility is an acceptable change.

The source document contains visual representations like pie charts and organizational diagrams (e.g., pages 14, 15, 29, 32), but it does not contain standard grid-based data tables. The Table of Contents on page 2 is correctly identified and rendered as a list rather than a corrupted table.

The reading order in the JSON follows the logical flow of the document. For example, on page 42, the title 'Looking Ahead' is placed at the start of its section despite its visual placement in the PDF, which is an optimization for accessibility. Sidebar/callout content (like on page 43) is integrated into the flow in a manner that preserves the meaning.

The Table of Contents on page 2 is correctly converted into an unordered list structure. Bulleted lists found throughout the document (though many are in omitted sections) appear to be handled as lists based on the visible elements.

The source PDF contains numerous internal and external links. For example, the Table of Contents (p2) links to pages, and there are external links to statutes (p4, p12, p29), executive orders (p9, p12, p26), and journals (p24). The JSON output fails to capture any of these links, providing only plain text for strings that are clearly hyperlinks in the source. This is a failure to capture visible link structures.
checks.b_evidence_source: Multiple links throughout the document, such as: 'N.C.G.S. 143B, Article 15' (page 4), 'Government Data Analytics Center (GDAC)' (page 7), 'Executive Order 254' (page 9).
checks.b_evidence_output: The text is present, but no hyperlink metadata or URL properties are included in the 'content' objects.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document, e.g., Element 17, Page 4.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the same visual organization and logical flow as the source PDF. Content is presented in the same order, starting from the cover, followed by the table of contents, and proceeding through the individual sections and feature articles. While the multi-column layouts of the PDF (such as on pages 7-8 and 24-25) are reflowed into a single-column layout in the HTML, this is an expected characteristic of responsive HTML conversion and does not hinder the user's ability to find information in its expected location.

The source document does not contain traditional grid-based tables. Data-heavy sections, such as 'Broadband by the Numbers' (page 19) or the 'Budget Report' (pages 14-15), which are presented as sidebars or charts in the PDF, have been functionally converted into lists or structured text in the HTML. This information remains readable and understandable to a sighted user.

Most meaningful images (photos and diagrams like the NC eLink graphic) are present. However, a significant meaningful graphic is missing: the 'Governance structure for the N.C. Health Information Exchange Authority' diagram from page 32. In the HTML output, the text and the caption for this figure are present, but the diagram image itself is absent between them. Additionally, the pie charts on pages 14 and 15 are missing as visual graphics, though their data is represented as text lists.
checks.b_evidence_source: On page 32, a complex flowchart/diagram appears above the caption 'The governance structure for the N.C. Health Information Exchange Authority'.
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML screenshot, under the 'HEALTH CARE' section, the text 'NCDHHS’ patient matching was enhanced...' is followed immediately by the caption 'The governance structure for the N.C. Health Information Exchange Authority' with no image rendered in between.

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of body content (paragraphs, data blocks) being visually duplicated in the HTML output. Section titles appearing in both the table of contents and the main body are intentional and expected accessibility features.
